text: The successful warrior is the average man, with laser-like focus.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Concentrate all your thoughts upon the work at hand. The sun's rays do not burn until brought to a focus.
author: Alexander Graham Bell

text: It is not enough to be busy. So too are the ants. The question is: What are we busy about?
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Where focus goes, energy flows.
author: Tony Robbins

text: The key is not to prioritize what's on your schedule, but to schedule your priorities.
author: Stephen Covey

text: You can do anything, but not everything.
author: David Allen

text: Focus is a matter of deciding what things you're not going to do.
author: John Carmack

text: Lack of direction, not lack of time, is the problem. We all have twenty-four hour days.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: The main thing is to keep the main thing the main thing.
author: Stephen Covey

text: Your mind is for having ideas, not holding them.
author: David Allen

text: The shorter way to do many things is to only do one thing at a time.
author: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart

text: Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task.
author: Cal Newport

text: Energy and persistence conquer all things.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: It always seems impossible until it is done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
author: William James

text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
author: Wayne Gretzky

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Push yourself, because no one else is going to do it for you.
author: Anonymous

text: Great things never come from comfort zones.
author: Anonymous

text: Don't stop when you're tired. Stop when you're done.
author: Anonymous

text: Success is not final; failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

text: With the right amount of momentum, ordinary people can do extraordinary things.
author: Anonymous

text: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
author: Anne Lamott

text: Rest is not idleness. Lying in the grass under trees on a summer's day is by no means a waste of time.
author: John Lubbock

text: Take rest; a field that has rested gives a bountiful crop.
author: Ovid

text: Sleep is the best meditation.
author: Dalai Lama

text: The time to relax is when you don't have time for it.
author: Sydney J. Harris

text: A good laugh and a long sleep are the best cures in the doctor's book.
author: Irish Proverb

text: Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself, your body, your mind, your spirit.
author: Ralph Marston

text: Tension is who you think you should be. Relaxation is who you are.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is rest.
author: Mark Black

text: Your calm mind is the ultimate weapon against your challenges.
author: Bryant McGill

text: Recovery is just as important as the work itself.
author: Anonymous

text: Resting is not laziness; it is the art of renewal.
author: Anonymous

text: The bow that is always bent will soon cease to shoot straight.
author: Anonymous

text: The present moment is the only moment available to us, and it is the door to all moments.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Wherever you are, be all there.
author: Jim Elliot

text: Mindfulness is simply being aware of what is happening right now without wishing it were different.
author: James Baraz

text: In today's rush, we all think too much, seek too much, want too much — and forget the joy of just being.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.
author: Buddha

text: The little things? The little moments? They aren't little.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

text: Breathe. Let go. And remind yourself that this very moment is the only one you know you have for sure.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: Life is available only in the present moment.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: You can't stop the waves, but you can learn to surf.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

text: Walk as if you are kissing the Earth with your feet.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Awareness is the greatest agent for change.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.
author: Rumi

text: Be where you are; otherwise you will miss your life.
author: Buddha
